LAW AND MOTION, 301, OFF CALENDAR. ADDED FOR POSTING OF PART 2 OF 2 OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. =(301/CVA)

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO SEP-04-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DELINDA MACUT AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED'S MOTION TO DISMISS INDIVIDUAL AND REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF DELINDA MACUT MOVES TO DISMISS THIS ACTION WITH PREJUDICE AS TO HER INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS AND WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO HER REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED FOR HEARING TO SEPTEMBER 25, 2025 AND MACUT IS ORDERED TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND DECLARATION AS STATED HEREIN (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Individual And Representative Paga Claims
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DELINDA MACUT AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED's Motion To Dismiss Individual And Representative Paga Claims.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to dismiss plaintiff Delinda Macut's individual claims against defendants with prejudice and her representative PAGA claims without prejudice is granted. Plaintiff's supplemental briefing and declaration demonstrate that her representative PAGA claims overlap with another earlier-filed action. The interests of absent aggrieved employees are therefore protected, and the court finds that this individual settlement is fair and reasonable to absent aggrieved employees.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Nov-05-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Individual And Representative Paga Claims
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Continued To Sep-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DELINDA MACUT AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED's Motion To Dismiss Individual And Representative Paga Claims.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Delinda Macut moves to dismiss this action with prejudice as to her individual claims and without prejudice as to her representative PAGA claims. The motion is continued for hearing to September 25, 2025 and Macut is ordered to file a supplemental brief and declaration as stated herein.

Plaintiff brought this case as a PAGA action alleging violations of Labor Code sections 2698 et seq. She has reached a settlement of her individual claims with the defendants. She contends that the parties in another case, Lawson v. Grubhub, Inc. (N.D. Cal.) No. 3:15CV05128, have reached a global settlement that "likely" encompasses the claims of aggrieved employees of this action. (Dosaj Dec. para. 3.)

Labor Code 2699, subdivision (s)(2), provides that "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part. The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." This case is a civil action filed pursuant to PAGA and therefore any settlement of it must be approved by the court and submitted to LWDA. Macut provides no proof that LWDA has been notified of her settlement and abandonment of her representative claims. Moreover, Macut provides insufficient information for this court to approve the settlement. There is no carveout in the statutory language where representative claims are dismissed without prejudice; what triggers application of the review provision is that the case was filed as a PAGA representative claim.

A trial court is required under sec. 2699(s)(2) to "scrutinize whether, in resolving the action, a PAGA plaintiff has adequately represented the state's interests, and hence the public interest." (Moniz v. Adecco USA, Inc. (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 56, 89 [disapproved in part on other grounds by Turrieta v. Lyft, Inc. (2024) 16 Cal.5th 664, 684].) If indeed the absent aggrieved employees' claims are resolved by Lawson, the settlement here may be fair and reasonable, and the state's interests adequately represented, but the court cannot ascertain that based on an assurance that another settlement "likely" covers those claims.

The court continues the motion for hearing to September 25, 2025. No later than five court days prior to the hearing, Macut is ordered to provide supplemental briefing including a declaration that attaches whatever documents from Lawson or other cases are necessary for the court to conclude that there is adequate consideration of the interests of absent aggrieved employees. Macut must also provide proof that LWDA has been notified.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Dhananjay S. Manthripragada, Esq. of GIBSON DUNN, T:213229.7366, for defendant/moving party;
Roy Suh, Esq. of THE KICK LAW FIRM APC, T:310.395.2988, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS GRUBHUB HOLDINGS, INC., GRUBHUB INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings as follows:

Defendants Grubhub Holdings, Inc. and Grubhub Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied.

The court stays this action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, 1281.4.

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
